vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of advanced collapse. The registry-defined specimens are largely absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot is empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Pothos and Rabbit anchor removed/missing)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Silver Guest)

plant_audit.P3: Pot removed from frame; anchor (rabbit) missing.
plant_audit.P4: Pot removed from frame; residual debris only.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Desiccated, dark, and devoid of organic growth.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshell fragments in the central pot, likely an amateur attempt at calcium supplementation or pest deterrent, now serving no functional purpose due to plant absence.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single leaf and rabbit anchor.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Progressive removal of structural elements and loss of remaining foliage.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Total biome clearance; only empty containers and debris remain.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total failure. There is zero photosynthetic activity. The environment is currently a 'dead zone' with no living plant matter present.
vision_report.anomalies: The removal of the scale anchor (white rabbit) indicates a deliberate decommissioning of the botanical observation station.
vision_report.narrative_description: The chronological sequence documents the rapid decline and eventual total abandonment of the indoor botanical setup. Starting from a state of minimal viability, the specimens have been removed or have perished, leaving only empty pots and scattered eggshell debris. The absence of the scale anchor confirms that the observation project has been terminated.
